basics.name: Yiyang Ma
basics.label: B.E. Candidate in Intelligent Manufacturing
basics.summary: Undergraduate researcher at Guangzhou University working on efficient visual modeling, reliable 3D visual perception, metric depth estimation, low-cost and sparse sensing, and edge AI deployment.

education.gpa: 3.60/4.0; average score 90.32/100; rank 4/57; comprehensive assessment rank 1/57 in sophomore year

work.company: Research Group of Prof. Xiaofei Yang, Guangzhou University
work.position: First Author; Leading Student Contributor - HALO
work.summary: HALO: A Sample-Adaptive Low-Rank Modulation Framework for HSI Classification. Manuscript in preparation for submission to Expert Systems with Applications (ESWA).
highlights: Identified the limitation of fixed-weight feature transformations in HSI classification, where complex intra-class spectral variability requires sample-adaptive modulation rather than uniform feature scaling.
Implemented a lightweight Transformer framework in PyTorch and contributed to Holographic Patch Embedding, Dynamic Low-Rank Adapter, and Spectral Polynomial Prior modules.
DLoRA introduces only 4.6K additional parameters per layer for sample-specific weight modulation.
Conducted ablation studies, visualization analysis, and manuscript writing.
Achieved 95.07% OA under 10% labels on Indian Pines and 86.22% OA on Houston 2013 under 1% labels, with 29.34M FLOPs.

work.company: Research Group of Prof. Xiaofei Yang, Guangzhou University
work.position: Second Author; Leading Student Contributor - R2Mamba
work.summary: R2Mamba: Route-Reliability Mamba for Hyperspectral Image Classification. Manuscript under review at IEEE JSTARS.
highlights: Developed an efficient Mamba-based HSI classification framework for patch-based central-pixel prediction under complex boundaries and mixed-pixel regions.
Designed the Route-Reliability Mamba Block, routing spatial token maps through row, column, main-diagonal, anti-diagonal, and center-outward Manhattan-ring scan paths.
Proposed a label-free local feature-difference cue and a Gate-Guided Aggregation head using path-gate entropy for certainty-aware pooling.
Conducted experiments on Indian Pines, Houston 2013, and WHU-Hi-HongHu with progressive ablation, boundary/interior diagnosis, local correction visualization, and route-fraction statistics.

work.summary: SSGTN: Spectral-Spatial Graph Transformer Network for HSI Classification. Published in Remote Sensing.
highlights: Contributed to implementation and evaluation of a dual-branch GCN-Transformer framework for label-scarce HSI classification.
Assisted with experiments involving adaptive superpixel graph construction, spectral-spatial shift modules, and complexity-accuracy comparison.
Achieved 99.62% OA on WHU-Hi-LongKou with 1% training samples while maintaining a favorable FLOPs-accuracy trade-off.

work.company: Marine Pipeline Inspection ROV Project Team
work.position: Lead Developer: Perception and Visualization Systems
work.summary: Marine Pipeline Inspection ROV with Real-Time Edge Perception and Digital Twin Visualization.
highlights: Built the perception and visualization software stack connecting the physical ROV, Jetson edge node, STM32 controller, and shore-side digital twin interface.
Implemented a real-time visual perception pipeline on NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano, including stereo image processing, CLAHE-based underwater enhancement, and YOLOv11 inference, achieving 38 fps.
Integrated a Flask and Three.js/WebGL-based monitoring dashboard for synchronizing depth, attitude, UWB position, motion status, video, and detection results on the operator side.
The system won the National 2nd Prize in the China Robotics and AI Competition and the National 3rd Prize in the National University Student Contest on Energy Saving and Emission Reduction.

work.company: Edge AI Smart Waste Sorting System Team
work.summary: Edge AI Smart Waste Sorting System with Three-Stage Differential Separation.
highlights: Built a 12,000-image waste dataset across 4 classes and 11 subcategories from approximately 8,000 self-collected and annotated images.
Trained a YOLOv5s model and deployed it on a K230 RISC-V edge board using ONNX, NNCASE, INT8 quantization, and channel pruning.
Reduced model size by approximately 60% with less than 1% accuracy loss and achieved 31 fps real-time offline inference.
Supported a Chinese invention patent application under substantive examination as the 3rd inventor.

work.summary: Lead one of the university's largest student innovation communities.
highlights: Coordinate technical workshops, project teams, and peer mentoring activities.
Organized Winter Camp and Summer Camp hands-on technology workshops with 500+ participants, promoting student projects in robotics, embedded systems, and AI applications.
